feature: Fix data extraction pipeline to 67% fill rate
workflow_rationale: While this addresses broken behavior, the implementation involves creating a new module (regex_extraction.py), adding smart truncation logic, wiring retry mechanics, and propagating extraction_model tracking — all net-new functionality layered onto the existing pipeline. Changes span 2 services (Python pipeline + TypeScript backend).

phases.name: Create Regex Extraction Module
phases.description: Create pipeline/regex_extraction.py with Romanian-pattern regex fallbacks for company, date, and address extraction. This is a standalone module with no dependencies on other changes.

subtasks.description: Create pipeline/regex_extraction.py with extract_company_regex(), extract_date_regex(), extract_address_regex(), and regex_extract() orchestrator function. Must follow codebase patterns: module docstring, logging.getLogger(__name__), %s log formatting. Company patterns: S.C. ... S.R.L./S.A., societatea ... S.R.L. Date patterns: valabil/expira/valabilitate keywords with DD.MM.YYYY or DD/MM/YYYY dates. Address patterns: Str/B-dul/Calea/Sos/Piata prefixes with sediul/adresa keywords. regex_extract() returns dict with all 6 standard fields; material and producator always None.

verification.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from regex_extraction import regex_extract, extract_company_regex, extract_date_regex, extract_address_regex; r = regex_extract('S.C. EXAMPLE S.R.L. valabilitate: 15.03.2026 sediul: Str. Test nr. 5', 'ISO'); print('company:', r['companie']); print('date:', r['data_expirare']); print('address:', r['adresa_producator']); print('material:', r['material']); assert r['companie'] is not None, 'company should not be None'; assert r['data_expirare'] is not None, 'date should not be None'; assert r['adresa_producator'] is not None, 'address should not be None'; assert r['material'] is None, 'material should be None'; print('ALL OK')"

phases.name: Smart Text Truncation
phases.description: Replace aggressive text[:6000] truncation with smart_truncate() that preserves header (2500 chars) + middle (2000 chars) + footer (1500 chars). This change is in extraction.py and is independent of the regex module.

subtasks.description:
Add smart_truncate(text, max_chars=6000) function to pipeline/extraction.py. Logic: if len(text) <= max_chars, return unchanged. Otherwise keep first 2500 chars (header), 2000 chars centered at midpoint (middle), and last 1500 chars (footer), joined by '

[...]

'. Replace the 'text[:6000]' on line 402 of extract_data_with_ai() with smart_truncate(text). Do NOT modify the _EXTRACTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT.

verification.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from extraction import smart_truncate; short = 'abc' * 100; assert smart_truncate(short) == short, 'short text unchanged'; long = 'H' * 3000 + 'M' * 3000 + 'F' * 3000; result = smart_truncate(long); assert '[...]' in result, 'separator present'; assert len(result) < len(long), 'truncated'; assert result[:10] == 'H' * 10, 'header preserved'; assert result[-10:] == 'F' * 10, 'footer preserved'; print('ALL OK')"

phases.name: Extraction Enhancement with Regex Merge and Model Tracking
phases.description: Enhance extract_document_data() in extraction.py to: (1) use regex as primary fallback when AI returns None, (2) always merge regex results into null AI fields, (3) set extraction_model field. Depends on the regex module being created first.

subtasks.description: Modify extract_document_data() in pipeline/extraction.py to: (a) Import regex_extract from pipeline.regex_extraction and AI_MODEL from pipeline.config. (b) If extract_data_with_ai() returns None, log warning and use regex_extract() as primary result, set extraction_model='regex_fallback'. (c) After normalize_extraction_result(), always run regex_extract() and merge: for each field where normalized value is None and regex value is not None, fill with regex value and log with logger.info. (d) Set normalized['extraction_model'] = AI_MODEL when AI succeeded, or 'regex_fallback' when regex-only. Set extraction_model AFTER normalization so it doesn't get wiped. (e) Use %s log formatting, not f-strings.

verification.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from extraction import extract_document_data; print('import OK')"

phases.name: Wire Extraction Retry in Pipeline Orchestrator
phases.description: Add single-retry logic to process_document() in pipeline/__init__.py: if all extraction fields are None and text >= 50 chars, retry extract_document_data() once.

subtasks.description: In pipeline/__init__.py process_document(), after extraction = extract_document_data(text, category), add retry logic: (1) Check if all values in extraction dict are None (skip 'extraction_model' key during check). (2) If all null AND len(text.strip()) >= 50, log with logger.warning('First extraction returned all null for %s, retrying...', filename) and retry: extraction = extract_document_data(text, category). (3) Single retry only — no loop. (4) Wrap retry in try/except like the existing extraction step. (5) Follow existing error handling pattern: if retry also fails, continue with null result (don't abort).

verification.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from pipeline import process_document; print('import OK')"

phases.name: Increase Backend Pipeline Client Timeouts
phases.description: Increase default and large file timeouts in the TypeScript PipelineClientService to prevent premature timeouts during longer AI extraction calls.

subtasks.description: In src/services/pipelineClient.ts, in the PipelineClientService constructor (line 126-128), change defaultTimeoutMs default from 30_000 to 60_000 and largeFileTimeoutMs default from 120_000 to 180_000. Only these two numeric values change — no other modifications to the file.
subtasks.notes: Changed defaultTimeoutMs from 30_000 to 60_000 and largeFileTimeoutMs from 120_000 to 180_000 in PipelineClientService constructor.

phases.name: Integration Verification
phases.description: Verify the complete extraction pipeline works end-to-end: smart truncation → AI extraction → regex merge → retry on all-null → extraction_model tracking → backend timeout handling.

subtasks.description: Verify all pipeline imports work correctly. Run the pipeline module imports to confirm no circular imports or missing dependencies. Check that regex_extraction.py integrates with extraction.py and __init__.py without errors.

verification.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from pipeline import process_document; from pipeline.extraction import extract_document_data, smart_truncate; from pipeline.regex_extraction import regex_extract; print('All imports OK')"

subtasks.description: Verify regex fallback works in isolation by testing regex_extract with representative Romanian construction document text samples. Confirm company (S.R.L./S.A.), date (valabilitate), and address (Str.) patterns all match.

verification.command:
cd pipeline && python -c "
from regex_extraction import regex_extract
test_text = '''CERTIFICAT DE CONFORMITATE\nS.C. TERAPLAST S.A.\nMaterial: Tevi PVC\nValabilitate: 15/03/2026\nSediul social: Str. Industriilor nr. 3, Bistrita, jud. Bistrita-Nasaud'''
r = regex_extract(test_text, 'CERTIFICAT_CALITATE')
print('companie:', r['companie'])
print('data_expirare:', r['data_expirare'])
print('adresa:', r['adresa_producator'])
assert r['companie'] is not None
assert r['data_expirare'] is not None
assert r['adresa_producator'] is not None
print('ALL OK')
"

subtasks.description: Verify smart_truncate preserves header+middle+footer content from a realistic long document. Test that documents under 6000 chars pass through unchanged and documents over 6000 chars include content from all three sections with [...] separators.

verification.command:
cd pipeline && python -c "
from extraction import smart_truncate
# Test 1: Short text unchanged
short = 'x' * 5000
assert smart_truncate(short) == short, 'short text should be unchanged'
# Test 2: Long text truncated correctly
long = 'HEADER_' * 500 + 'MIDDLE_' * 500 + 'FOOTER_' * 500
result = smart_truncate(long)
assert len(result) < len(long), 'should be shorter'
assert result.startswith('HEADER_'), 'header preserved'
assert result.endswith('FOOTER_'), 'footer preserved'
assert '[...]' in result, 'separator present'
print('ALL OK')
"

subtasks.description: End-to-end verification with Docker services. Start pipeline service and verify health endpoint responds. Then process a test PDF and verify extraction fields are populated and extraction_model is set.

steps: Upload a test PDF via POST /api/pipeline/process
Verify response has non-null companie or material
Verify response has extraction_model field set (not null)
Check pipeline logs for regex merge or retry messages

parallel_groups.reason: All three have no dependencies and modify different files: regex_extraction.py (new), extraction.py (truncation only), pipelineClient.ts respectively

parallelism.speedup_estimate: 1.3x faster than sequential — phases 1, 2, and 5 can run in parallel, but phases 3 and 4 are sequential and depend on previous phases

acceptance_criteria: All pipeline imports succeed without errors
regex_extract() returns non-null companie for 'S.C. EXAMPLE S.R.L.' text
regex_extract() returns non-null data_expirare for 'valabilitate: 15.03.2026' text
regex_extract() returns non-null adresa_producator for 'Str. Test nr. 5' text
smart_truncate() returns text unchanged when <= 6000 chars
smart_truncate() preserves header/middle/footer with [...] separators for long text
extract_document_data() sets extraction_model field in result
extract_document_data() falls back to regex when AI returns None
process_document() retries extraction when all fields are null
PipelineClientService uses 60_000ms default timeout and 180_000ms large file timeout
Pipeline health check still passes after changes

verification_steps.name: Pipeline Import Check
verification_steps.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from pipeline import process_document; from pipeline.extraction import extract_document_data, smart_truncate; from pipeline.regex_extraction import regex_extract; print('OK')"

verification_steps.name: Regex Extraction Unit Test
verification_steps.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from regex_extraction import regex_extract; r = regex_extract('S.C. TEST S.R.L. valabilitate: 01.01.2026 Str. Test nr. 1', 'ISO'); assert r['companie']; assert r['data_expirare']; assert r['adresa_producator']; assert r['material'] is None; print('OK')"
verification_steps.expected_outcome: OK — regex extracts company, date, address; material stays None

verification_steps.name: Smart Truncation Unit Test
verification_steps.command: cd pipeline && python -c "from extraction import smart_truncate; assert smart_truncate('short') == 'short'; long = 'x' * 10000; r = smart_truncate(long); assert len(r) < 10000 and '[...]' in r; print('OK')"
verification_steps.expected_outcome: OK — short text unchanged, long text truncated with separators

verification_steps.name: Docker Pipeline Health Check
verification_steps.command: docker compose up -d pipeline && sleep 5 && curl -s http://localhost:8001/api/pipeline/health

verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk pipeline enhancement. Core extraction logic is being modified but current fill rate is near-zero so regression risk is minimal. Unit tests for regex patterns and smart truncation are critical. Integration test via Docker health check confirms no import/startup errors.

commands: docker compose up -d pipeline && curl -s http://localhost:8001/api/pipeline/health
Upload test PDF via POST /api/pipeline/process and verify extraction fields

checks: SELECT extraction_model, COUNT(*) FROM extraction_results GROUP BY extraction_model — should show non-null values
SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE companie IS NOT NULL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) FROM extraction_results — should be >= 60%
